---
title: "Swapfiets Marketing Strategy"
description: "Group project developing a marketing strategy for Swapfiets bicycle subscription service in Karlsruhe, Germany."
author: "Woo-Sung Hollenhorst"
date: "2020-01-28"
image: ../../images/project4.jpg
categories: [Marketing, Market Research, Strategy]
---

<style>
body {
  background-image: url('../../images/background.jpg');
  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
  background-attachment: fixed;
}
</style>

## Overview

This group project was conducted in partnership with Swapfiets, the world's largest bicycle subscription service. Our team developed a marketing strategy to increase Swapfiets' customer base in Karlsruhe, Germany, with a focus on the winter months. We applied the Sinus-Milieus model for market segmentation, conducted primary survey research, and proposed guerrilla marketing campaigns targeting the Escapist milieu.

::: {.callout-note}
View the full report: [Swapfiets Report (PDF)](../../files/swapfiets-report.pdf){target="_blank"}
:::

## Market Segmentation: Sinus-Milieus

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

milieus = ['High Achiever', 'Socio-ecological', 'Escapist', 
           'Movers & Shakers', 'New Middle Class']
share = [27, 16, 15, 10, 8]
colors = ['#5bcefa', '#7c73ff', '#2ecc71', '#e74c3c', '#f39c12']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))
wedges, texts, autotexts = ax.pie(share, labels=milieus, colors=colors,
                                   autopct='%1.0f%%', startangle=90,
                                   textprops={'color': 'white', 'fontsize': 11})
ax.set_title('Sinus-Milieus Distribution in Karlsruhe', fontsize=14, color='white')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Survey: Interest by Milieu

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

milieus = ['Movers &\nShakers', 'High\nAchiever', 'Escapist', 'New Middle\nClass']
interest = [69.23, 64.29, 62.75, 55.00]
colors = ['#2ecc71', '#5bcefa', '#7c73ff', '#f39c12']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
bars = ax.bar(milieus, interest, color=colors)
ax.set_ylabel('Interest (%)')
ax.set_title('Interest in Swapfiets Subscription by Milieu')
ax.set_ylim(0, 100)
for bar, val in zip(bars, interest):
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2, bar.get_height() + 2,
            f'{val}%', ha='center', color='white')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Potential Market Population

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

groups = ['High Achiever', 'Escapist', 'Movers & Shakers', 
          'New Middle Class', '18-24 Year Olds', 'Chinese Students']
potential = [53728, 29314, 21429, 13619, 23137, 1780]
colors = ['#5bcefa', '#7c73ff', '#2ecc71', '#f39c12', '#e74c3c', '#95a5a6']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
bars = ax.barh(groups, potential, color=colors)
ax.set_xlabel('Potential Market Population')
ax.set_title('Estimated Market Potential by Target Group in Karlsruhe')
ax.invert_yaxis()
for bar, val in zip(bars, potential):
    ax.text(bar.get_width() + 500, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height()/2,
            f'{val:,}', va='center', color='white')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Winter Cycling Preferences

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

gadgets = ['Winter Tires', 'Earmuffs', 'Reflective Vest', 'Face Mask', 
           'Gloves', 'Rain Jacket', 'Water Bottle', 'Other']
escapist = [14, 17, 15, 4, 1, 8, 7, 2]
new_middle = [5, 3, 5, 8, 12, 5, 3, 3]
high_achiever = [13, 8, 8, 8, 6, 7, 8, 0]
movers = [1, 3, 5, 11, 5, 2, 1, 1]

x = np.arange(len(gadgets))
width = 0.2

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
ax.bar(x - 1.5*width, escapist, width, label='Escapist', color='#5bcefa')
ax.bar(x - 0.5*width, new_middle, width, label='New Middle Class', color='#7c73ff')
ax.bar(x + 0.5*width, high_achiever, width, label='High Achiever', color='#2ecc71')
ax.bar(x + 1.5*width, movers, width, label='Movers & Shakers', color='#e74c3c')
ax.set_ylabel('Number of Respondents')
ax.set_title('Winter Cycling Gadget Preferences by Milieu')
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(gadgets, rotation=30, ha='right')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Key Findings

- **Escapist milieu** was selected as the primary target group due to the greatest market potential (~29,300 people) and strongest alignment with Swapfiets' service
- **Chinese students at KIT** represented a high-potential niche with 90.91% interest rate, driven by an existing bike-sharing culture
- **Winter tires** were the most requested addition across all milieus, with 33 total responses
- Marketing strategy proposed poster campaigns and guerrilla marketing including mural art and a cycling parade

## Reflection

This was my first experience working with a company on a live business problem, through the university course. Leading a team of five through research, survey design, and strategy taught me how to combine different perspectives into one clear deliverable. Conducting street surveys in Karlsruhe gave me hands-on experience with primary data collection and its challenges. Our findings eventually led to the execution of a similar strategy by the company.
